title: castilleja ambigua var. ambigua
intro: Castilleja ambigua var. ambigua, commonly known as Mountain Indian Paintbrush, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright red and yellow flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Castilleja ambigua var. ambigua include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Castilleja ambigua var. ambigua can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
